name: No-Knead Refrigerator Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in the warm water in a large mixing bowl.
2. Add the sugar, shortening, eggs, mashed potatoes, and 4 cups of flour to the mixture. Beat for 30 seconds at low speed of an electric mixer, then beat for 2 minutes at medium speed, scraping the bowl occasionally.
3. Stir in the remaining flour until well combined. Place the dough in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover tightly and refrigerate for 8 hours or up to 3 days before shaping.
4. Divide the dough into two halves. Shape each half into a loaf and place them in two greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pans. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, for 3 hours or until doubled in bulk.
5. Bake at 400°F for 15 to 20 minutes or until the loaves sound hollow when tapped.
Yield: 2 loaves
Note: To make rolls, shape as desired. Place them in greased pans. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, until doubled in bulk. Bake at 400°F for 10 to 15 minutes. Yield: 2 dozen.
